biosimilars_in_market: Amjevita, Cyltezo, Hyrimoz, Hadlima, Yuflyma, Yusimry, Adalimumab-ADAZ, Adalimumab-FKJP
analysis_note: Adalimumab biosimilars launched Jan 2023; achieved 1.1% claims share by EOY. EU comparison: >80% biosimilar share by 2020.
mechanism: PBM formulary exclusion + high rebate incentives for brand originator

biosimilars_in_market: Erelzi (etanercept-szzs) — FDA-approved 2016, not in Part D 2023
analysis_note: Erelzi (FDA-approved 2016) has 0% Medicare Part D penetration through 2023; indicates PBM formulary exclusion.
mechanism: PBM formulary suppression despite FDA interchangeability

analysis_note: Semglee approved July 2021 as interchangeable; <2% claims penetration 4 years post-approval. Lantus maintained high-rebate preferred status.
mechanism: PBM maintaining high-rebate brand despite low-cost biosimilar availability

biosimilars_in_market: Zarxio, Nivestym, Granix, Fulphila, Ziextenzo, Udenyca, Nyvepria, Fylnetra
analysis_note: COUNTER-EXAMPLE: Zarxio launched 2015; achieved 76% claims penetration. Often provider-administered (Part B), reducing PBM formulary leverage.
mechanism: Functioning competitive market (unmanaged by PBM due to provider administration)
